response.justification: Strong senior IC with 20+ years building web, mobile, and embedded systems, plus genuine developer-tools work (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD setup, MCP code generators). However, the role requires a Level II people manager with 2+ years of formal people management and 3+ years in a technical leadership role, and the resume shows no evidence of direct reports, performance reviews, 1:1s, or org-change leadership. Mobile-platform ecosystem experience is also thin — some iOS/Apple TV work over 15 years ago. Fit as a Staff/Principal IC, not as an Engineering Manager.
strengths: 20+ years of polyglot engineering with deep dev-tooling output (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD, MCP tooling)
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, workshops and courses taught to other engineers
Founder of GISCollective — demonstrates long-term ownership, roadmap thinking, and cross-stack system design
concerns: No evidence of formal people management (direct reports, performance reviews, 1:1s, hiring) required for a Level II Manager role
Mobile-platform ecosystem experience is minimal and dated (iOS/Apple TV circa 2008–2009)
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or owning a team-level mid-term roadmap
